title: MySQL Replication Lag Causing Data Inconsistencies
symptoms: MySQL SHOW SLAVE STATUS showing Seconds_Behind_Master at 2,700
Read queries returning data 45 minutes stale compared to primary
Customer reports of order status not updating after changes
Relay log growing faster than it could be applied
Replica I/O thread running but SQL thread falling behind
root_cause: Large batch UPDATE on 8 million rows replicated as row-based binlog events, causing the single-threaded SQL applier on the replica to fall 45 minutes behind the primary
resolution: Temporarily routed EU read traffic to the primary, enabled parallel replication on the replica, chunked the batch operation for future use, and waited for replica to catch up
preventive_actions: Enable multi-threaded parallel replication on all read replicas
Implement batch operation chunking policy limiting to 100K rows per transaction
Add replication lag monitoring with alerts at 5-minute and 15-minute thresholds
Create read routing policy to exclude replicas with lag above threshold
Implement automated read traffic failover when replication lag is detected

timeline.event: Replication lag begins growing on mysql-replica-eu-02
Lag reaches 10 minutes, monitoring alert fires
Large batch UPDATE identified as cause of sequential replication
EU read traffic temporarily routed to primary
Parallel replication enabled on affected replica
Replica catching up with parallel processing
Replication lag at zero, read traffic restored to replica

embedding_summary: MySQL replication lag of 45 minutes on EU read replica caused by large batch UPDATE of 8 million rows processed sequentially. EU users saw stale data for 90 minutes. Resolved by routing reads to primary temporarily and enabling parallel replication.
